experiences.intentions:
RUSSIA: Russia starts with 4 centers and strong expansion chances to Rumania and Scandinavia. Main early threats are Turkey in the south and Germany/Austria in the center; England is a potential rival for Scandinavia but not immediately dangerous if I move to Gulf of Bothnia. Best plan is standard flexible opening: pressure/contest Black Sea from Sevastopol, move Warsaw to Ukraine to support into Rumania in fall or react to Galicia, move Moscow north to St. Petersburg/Livonia lane, and send StP fleet to Gulf of Bothnia for Sweden. This balances defense and expansion on key borders: Black Sea, Galicia/Ukraine, and Scandinavia.
ENGLAND: England starts with 3 centers and strong naval flexibility. Immediate priorities are securing Norway in the fall and avoiding early exposure to France/Germany. France is the main western naval threat, Germany is a potential rival or temporary partner, and Russia contests Scandinavia. Standard balanced opening is defensive-positional: fleet to North Sea, fleet to Norwegian Sea, army to Yorkshire. This maximizes fall options for Norway, Belgium, or home defense while not directly antagonizing France.
TURKEY: Turkey starts with 3 centers and a strong southern corner position. The key early objective is gaining Balkan access while avoiding immediate overextension. Russia is the main direct threat because of Black Sea/Armenia pressure; Austria is the main medium-term rival in the Balkans but can also be a useful anti-Russian partner. In Spring 1901, the most important border is the Black Sea/Bulgaria line. Best practical plan is to contest Black Sea with F Ank, take Bulgaria with A Con, and keep A Smy flexible while covering Constantinople from a bounce if needed by moving to Ank. That secures a likely build path without leaving Ankara exposed.
GERMANY: Germany starts with 3 centers and strong growth chances in Denmark, Holland, and Belgium. Immediate strategic priorities are securing Scandinavia/North Sea access while keeping options open in the west and guarding against Russia. England and France are the key western neighbours; both can be threats, but in Spring 1901 a standard, flexible opening is best. Russia is a medium-term concern in the east. I should attack/expand rather than defend passively: take Denmark with the fleet, send Munich to Ruhr to contest/support later gains in Holland/Belgium, and move Berlin to Kiel to preserve flexibility for a possible 1901 build pattern and avoid overcommitting east.
ITALY: Italy starts with 3 centers and the usual key decisions are whether to pressure Austria, hedge against France, or open east. Austria is the immediate neighbor most able to threaten Venice, but also the best potential ally against Turkey; France is a longer-term western concern, while Turkey/Russia matter via the Balkans. In Spring 1901, a standard flexible opening is best: send F Naples to Ionian to project into the east, move A Rome to Apulia to avoid blocking and prepare convoy options, and hold or cautiously position Venice. Since there is no evidence of an arranged Lepanto or anti-Austrian attack, holding Venice keeps the Austrian border secure while preserving flexibility for Fall.
AUSTRIA: Austria starts with 3 centers and a vulnerable central position. Key early aims are securing Serbia and contesting/pressuring Galicia/Rumania while avoiding immediate collapse to Italy. Russia and Turkey are the main eastern threats; Italy is a potential threat but can often be deterred by moving the fleet to Albania rather than exposing Trieste. Best balanced opening is the standard hedgehog-style set: take Serbia with Budapest, challenge Galicia with Vienna, and send Trieste to Albania for future pressure on Greece and defense against Italy.
FRANCE: France starts with 3 centers and the standard western opening choices. Germany is the key immediate land threat; England is a naval rival but not yet directly dangerous in Spring 1901. Italy is a secondary concern, mainly via Piedmont/Tyrrhenian pressure later. Best priority is to secure Iberia while contesting/covering Burgundy. A strong flexible opening is Brest to MAO for Portugal/Spain access, Paris to Burgundy to pressure Germany and deny a German move there, and Marseilles to Spain to guarantee a build while not overcommitting against Italy. Borders that matter most now are Burgundy and the Mid-Atlantic; this is balanced expansion with some anti-German positioning.

experiences.intentions:
ITALY: Italy has 3 centers and no builds yet; units are positioned for standard Fall expansion. The key goal is securing Tunis while preserving flexibility in the east. Austria is the main immediate neighbor to watch, but with no direct pressure on Venice this turn and Turkey/Russia also contesting Austria, Austria is more a potential partner or future rival than an urgent threat. France and Germany are distant for now. Best play is to attack/expand rather than defend: convoy A APU to Tunis with F ION, and move A VEN to TYR to keep options on Austria/Germany and avoid a passive stance in Venice.
GERMANY: Germany has 3 centers and three units, with no builds yet. Key tactical facts: England is strong in the north with F NTH and A YOR, so Denmark/Kiel are vulnerable; France has A BUR adjacent to Ruhr/Munich and is the main western land threat. Russia has F BOT and can contest Sweden, but I can at least force or bounce there. Best plan is balanced defense and gain attempt: move F DEN to SWE for a likely build chance if uncontested or bounced by Russia, keep A KIE supporting A RUH to hold against pressure from BUR/YOR, and move A RUH to HOL to grab a neutral if available while still not exposing Munich directly this turn.
ENGLAND: England has 3 centers and currently strong naval placement with F NTH and F NWG, but no gains yet. Main threats are Germany in Denmark/Ruhr and France in MAO/Burgundy; Russia is less immediate though StP can contest Norway. Best course is to secure a build by taking Norway while positioning the army for a possible convoy to the continent. Supporting NWG to NWY with NTH maximizes chance against Russian interference, and YOR to BEL via convoy contests Belgium while leveraging NTH’s convoy option. This is an attack-for-gains posture while still keeping naval control of northern waters.
FRANCE: France has secured Spain and Burgundy in Fall 1901 with only the original 3 home centers so far; the key objective is to gain at least one build, ideally Portugal, while preserving flexibility against Germany and England. Germany is the main immediate land threat with A RUH/A KIE adjacent, while England’s fleets in NTH/NWG are a looming naval concern but not an immediate attacker on French soil this turn. Italy is not pressuring us directly. Best plan is to take Portugal with support from MAO, keep Burgundy in place as a deterrent against Ruhr/Munich, and avoid overcommitting the fleet away from Iberia before securing the build.
TURKEY: Turkey is still on 3 centers with units in Ank, Smy, and Bul; the key objective is securing a fourth center this fall while not exposing the homeland. Russia is the main threat, with F SEV and A UKR both poised toward Black Sea/Armenia/Rumania. Austria is also nearby but currently better treated as a temporary counterweight to Russia, especially since A BUL can contest Rumania. Best plan is balanced offense/defense: take the strongest chance at a gain with A BUL - RUM supported by F ANK, while A SMY - ARM pressures Sevastopol and covers against Russian expansion. This contests Russia on both critical borders, avoids wasting tempo on CON shuffles, and keeps Turkish home centers reasonably safe.
RUSSIA: Russia has 4 centers and all four units active, with key fronts in the north and south. In the north, England/Germany contest Scandinavia; taking Sweden is the best growth chance, and moving A STP via convoy with F BOT to SWE maximizes pressure. In the south, Turkey is the main threat/opportunity; with Austria in Galicia and Turkey in Bulgaria, Rumania is highly contested. Using both A UKR and F SEV on RUM gives the best chance to secure it while keeping pressure on Turkey. Borders that matter most are Sweden/Baltic in the north and Rumania/Black Sea in the south. This is an attacking posture for gains in SWE and RUM rather than passive defense.
AUSTRIA: Austria has 3 centers and three units, with strong tactical access to two key fall gains: Greece and Rumania. Italy is the main immediate tactical threat because A Ven, A Apu, F Ion can contest Trieste/Greece/Albania; however Italy can also be cooperated with if they take Greece while I secure Rumania. Russia is a strategic threat in Galicia/Ukraine/Sevastopol and Turkey in Bulgaria/Ankara/Smyrna, but both can be played against each other. Given my current positions A SER, A GAL, F ALB, the best balanced plan is to secure two builds if possible while minimizing conflict with Italy: A SER - RUM supported by A GAL, and F ALB - GRE. Borders that matter most are Rumania, Greece, and Trieste/Albania. This is an attacking posture for growth, with enough spread to avoid conceding everything to neighbors.

experiences.intentions:
ENGLAND: England has 5 centers and 3 units, so gains 2 builds. Position is excellent: Belgium and Norway secured, with fleets in NTH and NWY giving strong northern control. Main threats are Germany adjacent to BEL/NTH and France in BUR/MAO; Russia is less immediate, though STP/BOT matters in the north. Priorities are to defend the island and preserve flexibility while preparing pressure on the continent. Build one fleet in London to reinforce naval dominance versus France/Germany, and one army in Liverpool to strengthen homeland defense and future continental operations. Borders that matter most are North Sea/Channel and Belgium-Holland.
TURKEY: Turkey has 4 centers and 3 units, so one build. Position: BUL gained, core secure, key fronts are Black Sea/Caucasus and Balkans. Austria is the main immediate threat with A SER, A GAL, F GRE adjacent to Balkans; Russia is also a threat with F SEV and A UKR pressuring Black Sea/Armenia. Need flexible defense and leverage in both theatres. A fleet at CON best contests Black Sea next year and supports Ankara/Bulgaria while existing A ARM handles east; building in SMY is slower to key fronts. Build rather than waive.
FRANCE: France has 4 centers and 3 units, so gains 1 build. Position is strong in the west with MAO, BUR, and POR, but England is the main immediate threat with fleets in NTH/NWY and an army in BEL; Germany is also adjacent with A HOL/A KIE/F DEN. Italy is less urgent right now despite TYR/TUN. The key borders are the Channel/north coast and Burgundy-Marseilles line. Since MAO can already influence Iberia/Atlantic and the biggest medium-term danger is Anglo-German pressure on the north, the best build is a fleet in Brest to contest ENG/IRI/Mid-Atlantic approaches and defend home centers while preserving flexibility.
ITALY: Italy has 4 centers and 3 units, so gains 1 build. Position is strong in the central Mediterranean with F ION and A TUN, while A TYR pressures both Austria and France/Germany. Austria is the main immediate land concern after growing to 5 and occupying GAL/SER/GRE; Turkey is a medium-term eastern naval concern; France is not yet directly threatening Italy’s homeland but western developments remain relevant. The key border is the Austrian front around Venice/Trieste/Tyrolia. Best is to add an army in Venice to contest Austria and keep flexibility against France/Germany if needed. A fleet in Naples or Rome is less urgent with F ION already active and no second build available.
GERMANY: Germany is on 5 centers, up to 5 units, and currently has strong forward positions in Denmark and Holland while retaining all three home centers. Main threats are England (F NTH, F NWY, A BEL) and France (A BUR, F MAO), with England the more immediate naval danger and France a land threat to Ruhr/Munich. Russia is less immediate despite F BOT. In builds, the key borders are the North Sea/Low Countries and Burgundy/Ruhr/Munich. Best posture is balanced defense with flexibility: build F BER to contest Baltic/Denmark/Helgoland against England/Russia, and A MUN to cover Burgundy/Tyrolia/Ruhr and support western operations. This is preferable to A BER because naval strength is urgently needed.
AUSTRIA: Austria is strong at 5 centers with three units on the Balkan/Russian front and gains of Serbia and Greece. Main threats are Italy from TYR/ION, Russia from UKR/SEV/BOT though UKR pressures Galicia less now, and Turkey with BUL/ARM/ANK. The key borders are Tyrolia/Trieste against Italy and Vienna/Budapest/Galicia against Russia, while the southeast needs continued pressure on Turkey. In builds, Austria usually wants armies for the Balkans and anti-Italian defense; a fleet in Trieste can help versus Italy but with ION already active and TYR adjacent, securing the land front is more urgent. Best is two armies: A VIE to cover Tyrolia/Galicia/Budapest flexibility, and A BUD to reinforce against Russia/Turkey and support Balkan operations.

experiences.intentions:
FRANCE: France has 4 centers and 4 units, with a split position: Atlantic forces in MAO/BRE and armies in BUR/POR. Main threats are England in BEL/NTH/LON and Germany in HOL/MUN/KIE/DEN; Italy is less immediate but TYR/VEN means Marseilles must be watched eventually. Best plan is balanced defense/offense: contest English access to the Channel, keep BUR active toward Belgium/Picardy, and improve the Iberian army by moving toward Spain while preserving Atlantic flexibility. Borders that matter most are ENG/PIC/GAS and the Burgundy-Germany frontier.
RUSSIA: Russia is stuck on 4 centers and has a strained position: no Rumania, no Sweden, and exposed southern and northern fronts. Main threats are Turkey in the south (A ARM, A BUL, fleets ANK/CON) and potentially Austria contesting Rumania via GAL/BUD/SER, while England/Germany pressure the north around Sweden/Baltic. Best practical gain is Rumania with coordinated force from Ukraine and Sevastopol, while covering Sevastopol from Armenia and improving northern posture by contesting Sweden/Livonia. I will attack south for RUM, support that attack, move StP toward Livonia for central flexibility, and send BOT to Sweden to contest/occupy if open.
AUSTRIA: Austria is on 5 centers with solid central coverage: armies in Vienna, Budapest, Galicia, Serbia and fleet in Greece. Immediate threats are Italy from Tyrolia/Venice against Trieste/Vienna, Turkey contesting the Balkans with Bulgaria/Constantinople/Ankara/Armenia, and Russia from Ukraine/Sevastopol. Since Trieste is currently open and vulnerable to Italy, the key border is the Italian front; secondarily I want pressure on Rumania/Bulgaria while not overexposing Vienna. Best balance is to cover Trieste with support, keep Greece active against Bulgaria, and use Galicia/Budapest to pressure Rumania while Serbia supports the Balkans.
ENGLAND: England has 5 centers with a strong northern/naval setup: NTH, NWY, LON, plus A BEL and A LVP. Key issue is balancing defense of the home island and Belgium against pressure from France (A BUR, F BRE, F MAO) and Germany (A HOL, A KIE, A MUN, F DEN, F BER). France is the more immediate western naval threat, while Germany is the main land threat to Belgium. Best plan is to secure the North Sea, cover Yorkshire/London against convoy or fleet pressure, and challenge Germany around Holland/Denmark while keeping Belgium active. I’ll defend core borders and make a probing attack on HOL with support, while repositioning LVP to Yorkshire for homeland defense.
GERMANY: Germany has 5 centers and a solid core, but is under immediate western pressure: England has A BEL and F NTH adjacent to HOL/DEN, while France has A BUR threatening RUH/MUN and fleets that could grow westward. Russia is distant for now with only F BOT as a northern concern, and Austria/Italy are not immediate enemies. Best plan is a defensive-leaning posture in the west while probing for tactical gains: bounce/contest Belgium with A HOL, cover Ruhr with A KIE, and support Munich to hold against Burgundy. In the north, move F DEN to HEL to better contest England and keep F BER flexible by moving to BAL to watch Russia and avoid awkward self-blocks.
ITALY: Italy has 4 centers and 4 units, with Tunis secured and armies split between Tyrolia, Venice, and Tunis. The key question is whether Austria is a target or partner: Austria is strong at 5 and adjacent, but Turkey is also growing and Austria currently occupies Greece, suggesting likely eastern fighting. France has Burgundy and fleets west but is not the immediate threat. Best course is a cautious anti-Austrian/pro-opportunist posture: pressure Trieste while not overextending, and use Tunis/ION to contest or take Greece. Borders that matter most are Trieste/Venice/Tyrolia in the north and Ionian/Greece/Albania in the east. I will attack for gains on Trieste and Greece, using support where available.
TURKEY: Turkey has 4 centers and 4 units, with BUL gained and no builds beyond parity. The key front is the Balkans/Black Sea. Austria is the biggest immediate rival with 5 centers and strong Balkan presence (SER, GRE, BUD, GAL, VIE), while Russia still has SEV and UKR but only 4 centers, suggesting possible pressure from the north. Italy's ION is a medium-term concern but not yet immediate. Best plan is a balanced anti-Austria posture while keeping pressure on Russia and contesting Black Sea. Taking RUM is attractive but likely contested by Russia/Austria; GRE is also possible but risks overextension. Securing BLA and positioning for SEV/RUM while maintaining flexibility in BUL is strongest. So: move F ANK to BLA with support from F CON, use A ARM to attack SEV, and keep A BUL holding to avoid exposing Bulgaria while watching Austria.

experiences.intentions: GERMANY: Retreat phase only. Germany has 5 centers and a solid core, but A HOL was dislodged by England, indicating England is the immediate western threat; France is also nearby but less directly relevant for this forced retreat. Retreating to KIE preserves the unit and keeps defense of a home center/coastal hub strong, whereas disbanding would unnecessarily reduce board presence. KIE is the critical border now because it anchors Berlin/Denmark and fleet coordination.

experiences.intentions:
AUSTRIA: Austria is on 5 centers with 5 units, holding the Balkans core but under major pressure from Italy and likely Turkey. Italy occupies TRI and GRE and has F ION/A TYR/A TRI, making the western and southern borders critical. Russia has A UKR/F SEV nearby but is less immediately dangerous than the Italian/Turkish squeeze. Best course is defensive containment while trying to regain TRI and preserve RUM/BUL. I will cover TRI with supported attacks, support RUM, and keep BUL active against Turkish naval pressure.
ENGLAND: England has 5 centers and a forward position in HOL with fleets in NTH/NWY/LON and army in YOR. Immediate threats are France (F ENG, A PIC, F MAO) and Germany (F HEL, A RUH, A KIE, A MUN), with France the more urgent naval danger because ENG threatens the home island while Germany threatens HOL and Belgium approaches. Best course is primarily defensive in the north sea/home waters while preserving leverage on the continent: retake/contest ENG, cover HOL against German attack, and keep YOR flexible against a landing or as support for anti-French action.
RUSSIA: Russia is down to 4 centers and has lost all neutrals; position is strained between a strong Austria/Turkey in the south and England/Germany in the north. The immediate threats are Turkey in the Black Sea/Armenia and Austria occupying Rumania, while England/Germany pressure Scandinavia and the north. Best plan is a balanced defense with tactical pressure: contest/retake Rumania from Austria/Turkey using UKR supported by SEV, keep SEV active into BLA to challenge Turkish naval control, use LVN to cover Warsaw, and pull SWE to Finland to preserve the northern fleet and guard StP rather than risk unsupported fights in BAL/NWY.
TURKEY: We are Turkey on 4 centers with A ARM, F CON, F BLA; position is cramped and exposed. Austria is the main immediate threat: it has A RUM and F BUL/SC plus Balkan armies, directly contesting our gains and threatening CON/ANK/BUL. Russia still has F SEV and A UKR, but Austria currently has the stronger forward posture against us. Italy is nearby in ION/GRE/TRI and could pressure AEG/Balkans, but not as urgent this turn. Best plan is defensive containment against Austria while preserving flexibility versus Russia. Key borders are BUL/CON/ANK and Black Sea. F CON should retake/contest BUL, supported by BLA; A ARM should cover ANK against any Austrian/Russian stab from RUM/SEV/BLA interactions. This is a conservative defense-first set to avoid losing a home center while challenging Austrian occupation of Bulgaria.
FRANCE: France has 4 centers and 4 units, with positions split between Atlantic pressure (F ENG, F MAO) and continental defense/offense (A PIC, A SPA). The main threats are England and Germany: England has strong northern fleets and armies in HOL/YOR/LON, while Germany has RUH/MUN/KIE/HEL and can pressure Belgium or Burgundy. Italy is less immediate though TYR is a long-term concern for Marseilles. Best plan is a balanced anti-English/anti-German posture: secure Brest/Atlantic, contest Belgium/Holland approaches, and improve army placement toward home centers. F ENG is vulnerable and useful attacking English waters; F MAO can support or cover Brest. A PIC should move to BUR to defend the core and threaten Germany. A SPA to MAR shores up the south against Italy and keeps flexibility. Overall this is a defensive consolidation with some pressure on England.
ITALY: Italy has 4 centers and 4 units, with a stretched position: Tunis secured, armies advanced in Trieste, Tyrolia, and Greece. The key issue is Austria, which is strongest nearby at 5 centers and has armies in Serbia, Budapest, Vienna, and Rum plus fleet in Bulgaria. Turkey is also active but less immediately exploitable from this position; Russia is distant. Austria is the main threat and also the best target, especially with my armies already on its borders. I should press against Austria while covering against easy tactical losses. A coordinated shot at Vienna from Tyrolia with support from Trieste is strong; Greece can pressure Serbia, and Ionian can support into Albania to improve Balkan position and keep flexibility versus Turkey. This is an attacking posture focused on the Austro-Italian border, the most important front.
GERMANY: We are Germany on 5 centers with a strong but exposed western posture: England sits in HOL with F NTH/F NWY/F LON, while France has F ENG and A PIC. Russia has F SWE and A LVN, so the Baltic is also contested. Immediate threats are England against KIE/DEN/HOL and France against RUH/MUN via PIC/ENG. Russia is a secondary concern but can pressure BER/KIE through SWE/LVN. Best plan is defensive-flexible: protect KIE/MUN, contest or cover DEN, and keep armies positioned against Anglo-French pressure without overcommitting east. HEL should support KIE to DEN to try to reclaim/secure Denmark; BAL supports KIE itself against northern attack; RUH supports MUN to BUR to contest PIC and gain western leverage; MUN attacks BUR to pressure France and avoid being bottled up.

experiences.intentions: AUSTRIA: Austria has 5 centers but only one dislodged unit to resolve. Italy is the main immediate threat, occupying TRI, GRE, and having dislodged A VIE; Russia is also nearby with UKR/WAR, while Turkey is contained for now. In retreat phase the priority is preserving unit count and placing the army where it can help recover home centers. Retreating A VIE to GAL keeps an extra army on the board and pressures both Russia and the Vienna/Budapest front; BOH is less useful and more boxed in toward German/Italian lines. Therefore preserve the unit with A VIE R GAL.
experiences.tasks: Gain the most supply centres in Diplomacy through strategic orders and alliances.

experiences.intentions:
AUSTRIA: Austria is down to 4 centres but has 5 units, so must disband 1. Position is precarious: Italy is the main immediate threat with A TRI and A VIE plus F ION/A GRE, while Russia and Turkey are also nearby but Turkey is weak and Russia is not yet in our core. Borders that matter most are the home centers and the Balkans. We should keep armies covering BUD/SER/RUM/GAL for land defense against Italy/Russia, and retain the fleet in BUL/SC to contest the eastern Med/Black Sea area. The least essential unit is A SER, since BUD, GAL, and RUM are more critical to defend the core and A SER is somewhat redundant with Italian pressure focused farther north.
GERMANY: Germany has 4 centers but 5 units, so must disband 1. Position is overextended in the west/north after losses; England is the main immediate threat with A HOL, F ENG, F NWY and likely pressure on Denmark/Kiel, while France in PIC/MAR is secondary but still dangerous. Russia is comparatively distant from current front lines. We should keep maximum defensive flexibility around home centers and the North Sea/Baltic. F HEL is key against England, F BAL covers Berlin/Kiel approaches, A RUH defends Ruhr/Kiel/Munich, and A DEN directly contests Scandinavia and protects Denmark. A BUR is the most exposed unsupported unit and hardest to sustain, so disband it to consolidate the German core.
ENGLAND: England has 6 centers and 5 units, so we get 1 build. Position is strong in the north/Low Countries with HOL and NWY secured, but Germany remains the main immediate threat with HEL/RUH/BUR/DEN/BAL adjacent to our continental gains. France is weakened but still has MAO/IRI, so western waters also matter. We should build for flexible defense and power projection on the island; the most important border is the North Sea/Channel axis versus Germany, while keeping options against France. A fleet in Liverpool best reinforces northern seas and can contest NWG/NAO/IRI, whereas an army is slower and Fleet Edinburgh is less flexible for the west. Build defensively with future attack potential.
ITALY: Italy has 7 centers and 4 units, so we get 3 builds. Position is excellent in the east: holding GRE, TRI, VIE, TUN with Austria and Turkey weakened. Main immediate frontier is the Balkans/Eastern Med against Austria/Turkey/Russia; France is distant and currently weak, while Germany/England are not immediate threats. Best plan is to consolidate eastern gains and project naval power. Build F NAP to pressure Ionian/Aegean/Eastern Med, A VEN to reinforce Trieste/Tyrolia line and keep flexibility versus Austria/Germany, and A ROM as an additional army for Balkan operations or home defense. A fleet in Rome is less efficient than in Naples; a fleet in Venice is less generally useful than an army right now.

experiences.intentions:
ITALY: Italy is very strong at 7 centers with an Austrian core occupation: TRI, VIE, GRE plus home centers and TUN. Main immediate threat is the eastern cluster: Austria still has BUD/GAL/RUM/BUL while Turkey has CON/BLA/ANK, so the Balkans matter most. In the west, France is weak and distant; Germany/England are irrelevant short-term. Best plan is to consolidate gains and press Austria without overexposing Italian home centers. Attack/position on BUD and SER, keep naval flexibility in Ionian/Tyrrhenian, and avoid letting Austria or Turkey break into GRE/TRI/VIE.
FRANCE: France is on 4 centers with exposed Atlantic and northern fronts: we hold Iberia via POR but lack Spain proper, while England is the main immediate threat with F ENG, F LON, F LVP, A YOR and strong naval presence. Germany is nearby in RUH/HEL but not yet directly on our home centers; Italy is booming in the east and less immediate. Priority is to contain England at sea and improve position on the continent. F IRI attacks ENG with support from MAO to challenge the Channel. A PIC goes to BUR with support from MAR to gain a key land pivot against Germany/England and improve homeland defense. This is a balanced attack-defend posture focused on the north and Channel while keeping the south flexible.
RUSSIA: Russia is stuck at 4 centers with pressure from both north and south. England is the main northern threat: F NWY plus our isolated F FIN puts StP/SWE at risk, while Germany also has F BAL/A DEN nearby. In the south, Turkey still has F BLA and Austria/Italy are crowding the Balkans, but our best immediate chance is to contest Rumania while staying flexible. We should defend core positions and make a practical bid into RUM rather than overextend north. A WAR to GAL with A UKR support pressures Austria and opens tactical options; F SEV to RUM with A UKR support would be ideal, but one unit cannot support both. Since Galicia is key to the whole southern frontier, prioritize taking/contesting GAL and send F SEV to RUM as an unsupported opportunistic move. In the north, F FIN to BOT is safest, preserving influence around StP and avoiding being bottled up.
ENGLAND: We are England on 6 centers with a strong northern/western position: secure home island plus BEL/HOL/NWY, but no fleet yet in NTH. Immediate threats are Germany from HEL/RUH/DEN/BAL contesting the Low Countries and North Sea, and France from MAO/IRI/PIC contesting the Channel and western waters. Russia is less urgent with only FIN in the north. The key border is the North Sea/Channel interface. Best play is to seize NTH with support, cover HOL against Ruhr, and pressure/contain France in the west while keeping flexibility. I’ll move LON to NTH supported by ENG and NWY, use HOL to support itself/adjacent? Since support self-hold isn’t legal in given list, hold HOL, keep YOR flexible on home defense, and send LVP to IRI to challenge French fleet presence.
TURKEY: Turkey is down to 3 centers and has lost Smyrna, so survival and position recovery are priority. Immediate threats are Austria in Rumania/Bulgaria and Russia in Sevastopol/Ukraine, while Italy is very strong and nearby in Greece/Ionian/Trieste/Vienna. With only three units, I should defend the core and try to contest Bulgaria rather than overextend. Black Sea is the key border: if I can support into Bulgaria while keeping flexibility around Constantinople/Ankara, I may regain a foothold. Best practical play is to attack Bulgaria from Constantinople with Black Sea support, while army Ankara holds to guard against a convoy/tactical incursion and preserve home defense.
GERMANY: We are down to 4 centers and have clearly lost Holland, with England very strong at 6 and adjacent in NTH/ENG/HOL/YOR/LON/LVP configuration; England is the primary threat. France is weaker and could be a counterweight to England. Russia is present in FIN/WAR/UKR/SEV and not immediately pressuring our core except via the Baltic. Our priority should be preserving KIE/BER/MUN and avoiding further losses while creating tactical chances. The key borders are HEL-DEN-KIE-BAL and RUH vs HOL/BEL/KIE. Best practical defense is to use A DEN and F HEL to cover KIE while A RUH contests HOL and F BAL stays flexible while supporting DEN or holding the east. Given available orders, a direct supported attack on HOL with RUH plus HEL is attractive if HOL is occupied by England and not heavily supported; DEN should hold to avoid self-bouncing key defenses, and BAL should support DEN to guard against pressure and maintain Baltic control.
AUSTRIA: Austria is down to 4 centers and has lost Vienna/Trieste to a very strong Italy, which is now the main existential threat with armies in TRI and VIE plus fleet ION and army GRE. Russia and Turkey are both present but smaller; they are threats mainly if they coordinate, yet either could also help check Italy. Our priority should be defense and preserving Rumania/Serbia/Bulgaria while trying to create tactical friction among our enemies. The most critical border is the Balkan line around SER-BUL-RUM and the inland line BUD-GAL. Since Italy in GRE can hit SER and fleet ION can support, while Russia/Turkey can pressure RUM/BUL, a flexible defensive setup is best. I will pull A BUD to SER to cover that center, use A GAL to support BUD-SER, hold A RUM to avoid overextension and keep a unit on the eastern front, and keep F BUL/SC holding to guard Bulgaria/Constantinople approaches.

experiences.intentions:
AUSTRIA: Austria is down to 4 centres and only one standing unit, with two dislodged units to resolve. Italy is the overwhelming immediate threat, occupying TRI and BUD and surrounding the Balkans; Russia and Turkey are also adjacent but secondary compared with Italian pressure. In retreat phase, priority is preserving units where they can still matter. Retreat F BUL/SC to AEG to keep a fleet alive and maintain some southern presence; disbanding would further collapse our position. For A GAL, retreating to VIE is strongest because it reoccupies a home centre and blocks deeper penetration, whereas BOH/SIL are exposed edge positions with less direct defensive value.
ENGLAND: We have 6 centers and currently 4 standing units plus 2 dislodged units to resolve. This is a retreat phase, so priority is preserving force count where possible. France is an active naval threat in ENG/MAO; Germany occupies HEL, BAL, DEN and has dislodged us in HOL, so both western neighbours are hostile. Key borders are the North Sea/Channel/home waters and the Low Countries. A HOL can only retreat to BEL or KIE; BEL would likely create awkward overlap with our center and is more exposed, while KIE preserves a foothold on the continent and pressures Germany, so retreat to KIE is best. F ENG can retreat to PIC/LON/BRE/BEL/WAL or disband; keeping the fleet is valuable, and PIC is the strongest aggressive/positional retreat, maintaining pressure on France while avoiding cluttering home centers. So preserve both units with retreats to KIE and PIC.
experiences.tasks: Gain the most supply centres in Diplomacy through strategic orders and alliances.

experiences.intentions:
TURKEY: We are down to 3 centers and have lost CON/SMY temporarily or at least are under intense pressure; current units are clustered around the homeland. Main threats are Italy and Austria in the Balkans/Aegean, with Russia also adjacent via Sevastopol/Ukraine but potentially usable against Austria. The critical borders are CON/AEG/BUL and the Black Sea. Best practical stance is defensive recapture: take CON if open/contested while preserving Black Sea leverage and keeping AEG from rolling us. A ANK can support a fleet move to CON, while F BLA can also support that move, giving strong odds to reclaim/hold Constantinople against likely AEG action.
ITALY: Italy is strongest locally at 7 centers, holding a stretched line from Tri/Bud/Gre to the central Med. Austria is weakened to 4 and split; Turkey is down to 3 but still has fleets in BLA and BUL/SC plus Ankara, making the Balkans the key front. France is distant but F MAO and A MAR mean western defense matters somewhat; however immediate danger is eastern counterplay around Greece/Trieste. Best plan is to consolidate Balkans and press Austria/Turkey while covering against tactical losses: take/contest Serbia and Vienna, hold Trieste securely, and improve fleet posture in ION. This is primarily an attack east with defensive supports on critical borders Tri/Gre.
AUSTRIA: We are down to 4 centers with exposed home territory: Italy occupies BUD and TRI and has strong forces in GRE/ALB/VEN/ROM/TYS, making Italy the main immediate threat. Russia is also dangerous with A GAL and A UKR adjacent to our core, while Turkey has F BLA and F BUL/SC contesting the southeast. Priority is defense of VIE and attempts to recover/contest BUD while not letting Turkey gain easy ground in the Balkans. A VIE should move to BUD to challenge the Italian occupation directly. A RUM supports VIE into BUD for strength 2 against likely Italian hold/support patterns. F AEG is best used to take CON, creating a retreat/position against Turkey and preserving relevance in the east rather than getting tied to weak supports.
RUSSIA: We are badly compressed at 4 centers with no northern army presence; England/Germany are dangerous in the north, but our only active tactical opportunities are in the south. Italy at 7 and sitting in BUD/TRI/GRE/ALB is the biggest immediate land threat, while Turkey in BLA/BUL/ANK blocks our fleet but is also vulnerable if Austria cooperates. Austria still has RUM and VIE, so a pragmatic anti-Italian/anti-Turkish posture is best. I want to preserve flexibility, avoid self-bounces, and pressure Rumania/Black Sea while keeping Galicia relevant against Vienna/Budapest. In the north, F BOT should improve defense by moving toward Livonia rather than getting trapped. In the south, I will contest/pressure RUM with support from SEV, keep GAL supporting UKR into RUM, and shift BOT to LVN.
GERMANY: We are down to 4 centers and have lost all mainland German home territory except the SC list still includes BER/KIE/MUN, but the actual board presence shows England occupying KIE with fleets in NTH/NWY/IRI/PIC and army YOR, making England the primary existential threat. France also pressures the west with F ENG and A BUR/F PIC adjacency, but England is more overextended into our core. Russia in BOT could be a tactical counterweight against England in Sweden/Baltic, so eastern border matters less than preserving/retaking position against England. Best practical fall plan is to secure/contest key northern spaces: try to take Sweden with A DEN supported by F BAL, while using A HOL to hit KIE and F HEL to support that attack. This maximizes chances to regain a home center and possibly gain Sweden, while not wasting moves on passive defense.
ENGLAND: We are England on 6 centers with a strong northern/western naval posture and an exposed continental army in Kie plus fleet in Pic. Immediate threats are France (F ENG, F MAO, A BUR, A MAR) and Germany (F HEL, F BAL, A DEN, A HOL). France is the more direct naval threat to home waters and Belgium/Picardy; Germany blocks our position in Kie and can contest North Sea approaches. Russia is distant but F BOT could matter later; Italy is growing but not an immediate border issue. Best plan is balanced defense with tactical pressure: retake/contest the Channel, keep North Sea secure, protect Norway, and avoid overextending Kie. Use F PIC with F NTH to attack ENG, while IRI cuts/supports western pressure by moving to MAO. A YOR holds as home defense/flexibility. A KIE supports NTH into HEL to challenge Germany and improve continental leverage. F NWY supports NTH to hold in case of counterplay.
FRANCE: We are France on 4 centers with units F MAO, F ENG, A BUR, A MAR. England is the primary threat: it has 6 centers and fleets in NTH, IRI, and PIC plus A YOR, directly menacing BRE/PAR and the Channel coast. Germany is weakened but still adjacent in HEL/HOL/DEN; however, immediate danger is the English fleet in PIC and possible attacks on BRE/BEL. Italy is large but not yet on our critical borders except potential pressure on Piedmont/Marseilles. We should prioritize defense of home centers while trying to dislodge or contest PIC and keep tactical flexibility. Best is to attack PIC with strength 2 from ENG supported by BUR, while MAO supports ENG to hold against likely counterattack from IRI/MAO area is impossible so MAO can support ENG hold. MAR supports BUR to prevent opportunistic German/Italian pressure and keeps MAR safe. This is a defensive anti-English posture on the northern border, our most important front.
experiences.tasks: Gain the most supply centres in Diplomacy through strategic orders and alliances.

experiences.intentions:
AUSTRIA: Austria is down to 4 centers with only F AEG and A VIE on board plus dislodged A RUM. Italy is the main existential threat, occupying TRI/BUD/SER and surrounding the Balkans; Russia and Turkey are secondary concerns, but keeping a unit alive in BUL preserves Balkan presence and future tactical options. In retreat phase the choice is only disband or retreat; retreating to BUL is clearly superior because BUL is one of our centers and maintaining unit count is critical.
ENGLAND: Retreat phase only. England is on 6 centers and still well-positioned in the north and Low Countries. Main threats are Germany in HEL/HOL/SWE/BAL and France in MAO/BUR/MAR. The dislodged fleet in Picardy cannot safely aid pressure if disbanded, but retreating to Belgium preserves a unit and keeps leverage on the continent; Belgium is one of our centers and likely now open due to the dislodgement. Retreat to Brest would be overextended and vulnerable to French recapture. Preserve force and defend the Channel/Low Countries front.
experiences.tasks: Gain the most supply centres in Diplomacy through strategic orders and alliances.

experiences.intentions:
ITALY: Italy is at 8 centers with 7 units, so we gain 1 build and are in a strong eastern position holding the Balkans plus Trieste. Immediate threats are Russia and Turkey around the Balkans and Black Sea, while Austria is nearly eliminated and less dangerous; western powers are distant for now. The key border is the Ionian/Adriatic-Balkans axis, so the best build is another fleet to reinforce naval control and project toward the eastern Mediterranean while still covering the peninsula.
AUSTRIA: Austria is down to 2 centers but has 3 units, so must disband 1. Italy is the overwhelming immediate threat, occupying former Austrian home centers (TRI, BUD) and adjacent SER/ION/ALB; Russia and Turkey are secondary concerns. We should preserve the units with best defensive and recapture potential: A VIE is essential to hold the last home center and contest BUD/GAL/TRI, and A BUL can pressure/defend the Balkans. F AEG is more peripheral and trapped between stronger Turkish/Italian naval presence, so disband it.
GERMANY: Germany has 5 centers and 4 units, so one build is available. England is the main immediate threat with A KIE, F BEL, F NTH, and F NWY; France is secondary with A BUR/F PIC. Our current units are stretched north and west, and the key borders are KIE/HOL/DEN and MUN against BUR/KIE pressure. A build should prioritize land defense and flexibility against both England and France. Building A MUN best covers BUR, RUH, KIE, and BER, while F BER is less useful immediately and A BER is slower to contest the critical southern-western front.
RUSSIA: Russia has grown to 5 centers and has one build. Position is split: south units in Galicia/Rumania/Sevastopol face a dominant Italy and still-lurking Turkey, while north is thin with only Fleet Livonia against strong England and active Germany in the Baltic/Sweden area. Immediate strategic danger is the south: Italy has 8 centers and armies in Trieste, Budapest, Serbia plus fleets in Albania/Ionian, making the Austrian vacuum highly likely to turn against Rumania/Sevastopol. Turkey still has Black Sea/Constantinople/Ankara and can coordinate with or be overrun by Italy. In the north, England is strong but not yet on Russian home centers; Germany has Baltic and Sweden, so StP is also exposed. Since winter build placement should address the most urgent front while preserving flexibility, building A WAR is best: it reinforces Galicia/Rumania quickly, can cover Moscow if needed, and is more generally useful than a northern fleet build into a crowded Baltic where Germany/England already dominate. Attack posture is premature; priority is defense and central reinforcement against Italy/Turkey while keeping options north later.
experiences.tasks: Gain the most supply centres in Diplomacy through strategic orders and alliances.

experiences.intentions:
FRANCE: France is stuck on 4 centers with exposed western coasts and a forward army in Burgundy. England is the main immediate threat: fleets in IRI/NTH/BEL and an army in YOR mean pressure on ENG/Brest and Belgium, while Germany is adjacent but also under English pressure and can be a temporary counterweight. Italy is huge but distant from my core this turn; the key borders are the Channel, Picardy/Burgundy, and keeping Marseilles covered. Best plan is a defensive-anti-English posture while probing Belgium with support, preserving coverage of Marseilles and western waters.
TURKEY: Turkey is reduced to 3 centers and boxed into its home area with F BLA, A ANK, F CON. The key threats are Russia in Sevastopol/Rumania and especially Italy, which is very strong and adjacent via Aegean/Ionian/Balkans; Austria is weak and unlikely to help much. We should prioritize defense of Constantinople/Ankara while contesting Black Sea access and avoiding self-bounces that open lines. Best practical set is to hold Black Sea and support it from Ankara, while sending Constantinople to Aegean to watch for/contest Italian naval advance and create future options.
GERMANY: Germany has 5 centers and a stretched but defensible position: Munich anchors the south, Holland/Heligoland the west coast, and Sweden/Baltic the east-north. The biggest immediate threat is England, which has fleets in NTH/IRI/BEL plus an army in KIE and can pressure HOL, DEN, and the German core. France in BUR/PIC is a secondary western threat but currently less dangerous than England’s forward units. Russia is not pressing us directly and can be treated as a temporary non-priority. The key border is the north sea-Denmark-Kiel-Holland line. Best plan is defensive containment of England while creating tactical pressure on KIE and DEN. Munich should support pressure on KIE; BAL can support SWE into DEN while HEL attacks KIE. HOL can cut/support by moving to BEL, contesting England’s coastal position and avoiding being a static target.
RUSSIA: We are on 5 centers with a compact but pressured eastern position: WAR, MOS, STP, SEV, and forward RUM, with units split between north (LVN, WAR) and south (SEV, RUM, GAL). Italy is the biggest immediate threat/kingmaker in the south after swallowing most of Austria; Turkey is still alive with BLA/CON/ANK and can hit SEV/RUM. England and Germany are both strong in the north, so we should avoid overcommitting there while preserving STP/WAR approaches. Best plan is balanced defense with tactical pressure: secure RUM/SEV against Turk/Italian shots, while using GAL+RUM to pressure BUD and keep Italy from rolling east. In the north, WAR to PRU with LVN support contests German/English influence and may gain position without risking home centers.
ITALY: Italy is strong on 8 centers with a dominant Balkan position: TRI, BUD, SER, ALB, ION, plus the peninsula. The main immediate threat is Russia (GAL/RUM/SEV) contesting the Balkans and potentially Austria's remnants creating tactical nuisances around VIE/BUL. Turkey is weakened to 3 centers and can be pressured from ION/ALB/SER, while France is distant and not yet urgent. Best plan is to keep western defenses reasonable while attacking east: take/contest Greece, pressure Rumania, and improve fleet position toward the eastern Med without overexposing Venice/Trieste. Use supports to secure gains and maintain the Balkan line.
AUSTRIA: Austria is in catastrophic shape with only VIE and BUL remaining; survival depends on preserving both units and trying to regain a nearby center. Italy is the main existential threat, occupying TRI/BUD/SER/ALB and surrounding Vienna and Bulgaria. Russia is also dangerous with GAL and RUM adjacent, but Russia and Italy may compete. Turkey is weakened but has BLA/CON/ANK and could contest Bulgaria; however BUL is more immediately useful attacking Serbia than tangling east. Best practical chance is to cut into the Italian line: A BUL attacks SER, a key adjacent supply center, while A VIE supports that attack via VIE S A BUL - SER. This avoids exposing Vienna to an unsupported move and gives a realistic shot if Italy misorders or Serbia moves/supports elsewhere.
ENGLAND: We are on 6 centers with a split position: strong seas around the British Isles plus a forward army in Kiel and fleet in Belgium. Germany is the main immediate threat with HEL, BAL, HOL, SWE, MUN adjacent to our holdings; France has MAO/PIC/BUR but is more a secondary tactical factor unless they push ENG/BEL. Russia is distant but can pressure Scandinavia; Italy is growing fast but not an immediate border threat. Best plan is to pressure Germany while protecting home waters. Attack HOL from BEL with support from NTH and strike MUN from KIE with support from BEL unavailable, so KIE attacks directly while YOR covers/pressure via convoy route is unnecessary; instead YOR supports NTH or holds homeland. Use IRI to challenge MAO/cover western approach, NWY to secure north and support NTH if needed. Prioritize taking/contesting HOL and maintaining naval control of NTH/ENG approaches.
experiences.tasks: Gain the most supply centres in Diplomacy through strategic orders and alliances.

experiences.intentions:
GERMANY: Germany has 5 centers and is under direct pressure in the west: A HOL was dislodged, while England has F HOL and strong northern fleets; France is also adjacent via BEL/PIC/ENG. Russia is a secondary concern in the east with A PRU and F LVN, but this phase is only a retreat. Retreating A HOL to RUH preserves the unit and creates immediate defensive/offensive flexibility against BEL/HOL/KIE/MUN, whereas disbanding would drop me to 4 units and weaken my central line. RUH is the only legal retreat, so preserving force is best.
ENGLAND: Retreat phase only. England has 6 centers and still needs to preserve continental leverage after losing A KIE. Main threats are France in ENG/BEL area and Germany/Russia on the north continental front; Italy is growing fast but distant for now. Since KIE is no longer an SC we own, the key is keeping an army alive for pressure in Germany. Retreating to RUH is stronger than BER: RUH borders HOL/BEL/MUN/KIE and can influence both German and French fronts, while BER is more exposed to Russian/German pressure and less flexible. So preserve the unit with A KIE R RUH.
experiences.tasks: Gain the most supply centres in Diplomacy through strategic orders and alliances.

experiences.intentions:
ENGLAND: England has 6 centers and a strong northern/western naval position: NWY, NTH, MAO, HOL, plus A YOR. Immediate threats are France adjacent in ENG/PIC/BEL and Germany adjacent in KIE/DEN/BAL, while Russia is a secondary northern concern. The key border this turn is the North Sea/Low Countries/Channel axis. I should prioritize defense of my held centers and try to recapture Belgium from France if possible, while also contesting the Channel and improving MAO pressure on France. Best tactical shot is F HOL->BEL with support from F NTH, while F MAO attacks ENG to challenge French naval presence. F NWY supports NTH to prevent disruption from France/Germany/Russia, and A YOR holds as homeland defense since no clearly superior convoy attack is available.
TURKEY: Turkey is down to 3 centers and badly compressed; our immediate position is defensive with F BLA, A ANK, and F AEG covering the homeland. Italy is the biggest threat in the east with fleets in ION/GRE/TYS and multiple armies in the Balkans, while Russia also borders us via SEV/RUM/GAL. Austria is weak and not a major danger. Best course is to defend key borders and try to preserve flexibility around Constantinople/Black Sea. Supporting AEG to CON with ANK and BLA gives a strong chance to retake or secure CON if open/contested, while keeping fleet presence adjacent to home centers.
FRANCE: France is in a very weak 4-center position with exposed western seas and no unit on the home centers except A MAR. England is the immediate existential threat: F ENG adjacent to our coast, plus F MAO and strong northern fleets. Germany is a secondary concern around Belgium/Ruhr/Holland, while Italy is growing but not yet directly pressuring Marseilles beyond TYS. Best plan is to prioritize defense of Brest/Channel approaches while trying to regain a home center. Since F ENG can support F PIC to BRE or convoy attacks, the strongest practical move is F PIC - BRE with F ENG S F PIC - BRE to try to recapture Brest, while A BEL supports A MAR - BUR to improve our land position versus Germany and A MAR advances to BUR. This balances immediate home-center recovery with some continental leverage.
RUSSIA: We are on 5 centers with exposed fronts in both north and south. Italy is the biggest immediate threat in the Balkans, having absorbed Austria and massed armies in TRI/VEN/BUD/SER plus fleets in ION/GRE/TYS. Turkey is smaller but still contests the Black Sea with F BLA, so SEV must stay secure. In the north, Germany and England are both active; F BAL and A DEN threaten PRU/LVN/STP, while England's F NWY pressures STP. Best plan is mostly defensive while probing for gains: secure RUM against Italian/Turkish pressure, contest or take BLA from Turkey, and improve northern position by moving LVN to BAL and PRU to SIL while GAL pressures/attacks BUD. This keeps flexibility against Italy and Germany and avoids overextending.
GERMANY: We are on 5 centers with 4 units, missing Berlin and exposed on multiple fronts. England is the biggest immediate threat with F HOL adjacent to KIE/DEN and strong naval presence (NTH, NWY, MAO), while France has A BEL and F PIC/ENG and could pressure Ruhr/Kiel. Russia is also a concern in the east with A PRU and F LVN, but England/France are more immediately positioned to hit our core. Best play is largely defensive: cover KIE and BER approaches, avoid losing Denmark, and improve Baltic posture by retaking/holding Sweden if possible. A DEN to SWE is attractive because DEN is not a center and SWE is ours; supporting it from BAL secures against Russian or English interference from adjacent spaces, while KIE can support DEN to hold if attacked. MUN should hold to guard the south against any French move toward Ruhr/Burgundy/Tyrolia complications.
AUSTRIA: Austria is in a desperate 2-center position with only Vienna and Bulgaria. Italy is the overwhelming immediate threat, occupying former Austrian cores (Tri, Bud, Ser, Gre) and surrounding Vienna; Russia is also adjacent in Galicia and Rumania. Turkey is weaker and could be a temporary counterweight to Russia/Italy. We need to preserve our last home center while trying for a tactical gain or at least avoid easy elimination. Vienna’s key border is Galicia/Budapest/Trieste, with Galicia the most dangerous Russian launch point; Bulgaria borders Serbian/Rumanian/Greek/Turkish theaters. Best practical stance is defensive with a small opportunistic poke: hold or contest against Russia while not helping Italy. A VIE to GAL can at least challenge the Russian unit’s space and potentially bounce/support disruption, while A BUL to SER pressures an Italian-held center and avoids passivity. Even if unsupported, it is the most useful aggressive attempt from our position.
ITALY: Italy is strong at 8 centers, having absorbed much of Austria, but our eastern gains are exposed to Russia (GAL/RUM) and Turkey (BLA/AEG/ANK), while the west is comparatively quiet with France contained and no immediate threat to the peninsula. The critical borders are Balkans and the Ionian/Aegean line. Best plan is a cautious eastern attack/containment: pressure Vienna to finish Austria, contest Bulgaria from Greece/Serbia, and improve fleet posture by taking Aegean while keeping Tyrrhenian available versus France.
experiences.tasks: Gain the most supply centres in Diplomacy through strategic orders and alliances.

experiences.intentions: FRANCE: France is on 4 centers with only three units currently on the board plus a dislodged army in Belgium. The immediate western threat is England, which has strong naval presence and occupies BEL with a fleet after dislodging us; Germany is adjacent in Ruhr/Munich and can be either a danger or a useful counterweight to England. In this retreat phase, preserving the army is preferable to disbanding because every unit matters when already down in tempo. Retreating to Picardy is best: it keeps pressure and defensive flexibility against English holdings in Belgium/Channel and protects Paris/Brest. Retreating to Ruhr would overextend into a likely hostile German sphere and leave the homeland less covered.
experiences.tasks: Gain the most supply centres in Diplomacy through strategic orders and alliances.

experiences.intentions:
ITALY: Italy has 9 centers and 8 units, so we get 2 builds. Our position is strongest in the Balkans/eastern Med with armies in TRI/BUD/BUL and fleets in GRE/AEG/TYS; Austria is nearly gone and Turkey/Russia are the main immediate eastern concerns, while France/England/Germany are more distant for now. The key borders are Ionian/Tyrrhenian for western flexibility and the Balkans/Aegean for pressure on Turkey and Russia. Build one fleet in Naples to project into ION/TYS and support eastern or western operations, and one army in Rome to reinforce the peninsula or move north/east as needed. Venice is occupied, so no build there.
GERMANY: Germany has 6 centers and 4 units, so gains 2 builds, but only BER is open for building. Main threats are the western powers: England has fleets in NTH/BEL/NWY/MAO and France has ENG/BRE plus armies in BUR/PIC, so pressure on the north coast and Ruhr/Holland axis is likely. Russia borders via SIL/LVN but is less immediate than the Anglo-French naval presence. We should maximize defensive flexibility on the homeland front; with only BER available, an army there best contests SIL/PRU/MUN/KIE and supports western defense. The second build is impossible due to no other vacant home centers, so waive.
experiences.tasks: Gain the most supply centres in Diplomacy through strategic orders and alliances.

experiences.intentions:
ENGLAND: England has 5 centers and is under immediate pressure in the Channel/North Sea theatre. France is the main tactical threat with F ENG, F BRE, A PIC, A BUR poised against BEL/NTH and potentially the home island. Germany is also strong, but with France already in ENG the western front is the critical border. Best plan is defensive-containment: retake/secure NTH, protect BEL, and use MAO to cut or contest ENG while A YOR pressures PIC via convoy if possible. Holding Belgium while preventing a French breakthrough matters more than speculative northern moves.
AUSTRIA: Austria is nearly eliminated: only A VIE and 1 supply center remain. Immediate neighbors are overwhelmingly hostile—Italy occupies BUD and TRI and is the main threat, while Russia is in GAL/RUM and also dangerous. Germany in MUN/SIL could matter, but Italy is the existential enemy because it holds Austrian home centers. Best practical play is to try to retake BUD if Russia and Italy clash there; moving to BUD is our only direct chance to regain a center this year, whereas holding in VIE likely just delays elimination.
RUSSIA: We are a reduced Russia on 5 centers with exposed fronts in both the south and north. Italy is the biggest immediate threat: it dominates the Balkans with armies in TRI/BUD/BUL and fleets in GRE/AEG/NAP/TYS, so holding Rumania/Sevastopol and contesting BUD/VIE matters most. Turkey is weak but its Black Sea fleet blocks our southern fleet. Germany is the other major concern in the north/center with units in BER/MUN/SWE/BAL/KIE, while Austria is nearly dead and can be exploited. Best plan is a balanced defense with opportunistic pressure: support A RUM into BUD using A GAL, move SIL to BOH to pressure Vienna/Munich, hold SEV against Black Sea/Turkish pressure, and use LVN to PRU to contest Germany rather than sit passively.
FRANCE: France is on 4 centers with units concentrated in the north/west: F ENG, F BRE, A PIC, A BUR. Immediate danger is England, which still has F MAO, F BEL, A YOR, F NTH and can pressure both Brest and the Channel/Belgium coast. Germany is sizable but not directly on our home centers this turn; A MUN exists, but our key front is the Atlantic/Channel. Best plan is defensive-positional: secure Brest, contest MAO, and keep pressure on Belgium while covering Picardy/Burgundy from English incursion. F ENG to MAO attacks England's fleet there; F BRE supports to give strength 2. A BUR to BEL is an opportunistic attack on the English fleet/center, supported by A PIC. This balances attack on England with defense of our core borders.
TURKEY: We are reduced to our 3 home centers with only F BLA, A ANK, F CON, so survival and preserving flexibility matter most. Italy is the biggest immediate threat: it has fleets in AEG and GRE plus an army in BUL, directly pressuring CON/SMY/ANK. Russia is also adjacent with F SEV and A RUM, but Italy is the more dangerous western pressure and Russia can incidentally check Italy. Best plan is defense-first: contest/cover Constantinople and keep Black Sea useful. Ordering F CON to BLA prevents an Italian-supported attack on CON from AEG and can succeed if BLA vacates. F BLA to ANK provides direct supportable defense of ANK/CON and avoids being dislodged by stronger combinations in BLA. A ANK holds as the anchor. This setup maximizes chances to retain all centers against the immediate Italian fleet pressure.
ITALY: We are strongest on the board at 9 centers, with a large Balkan/Eastern position: BUD, TRI, BUL, GRE, plus fleets in AEG/TYS/NAP. Austria is nearly dead and not the main threat; Russia and Turkey are the immediate eastern opponents, while western powers are distant. The key border is the Balkans/Black Sea line. Best plan is to press Turkey for CON/SMY while also contesting RUM and covering against Russian counterplay. I will use AEG to attack CON, support it from BUL, push BUD to RUM with support from GRE via BUL holding, keep APU/NAP/TYS secure in the west, and keep VEN/TRI positioned without self-bouncing.
GERMANY: We are a 6-center Germany with a compact core but surrounded on all fronts. Immediate threats are west: England has F BEL/F NTH and France has F ENG/A BUR/A PIC, putting HOL/DEN/KIE/MUN under pressure. East: Russia has A SIL and F LVN, threatening BER/PRU/BAL/SWE. Italy is huge but not adjacent enough to be the immediate concern except via pressure on MUN through TYR/BOH later. Best plan is a balanced defense with tactical counterplay: preserve Munich against BUR/SIL, keep Berlin covered against SIL and possible KIE shenanigans, and contest Denmark while not overexposing. Supporting A BER to KIE protects KIE if attacked and keeps northern line coordinated; A MUN supports BER->KIE to prevent a western breakthrough. A SWE->DEN with F KIE support gives us a chance to take/secure Denmark against English naval pressure. F BAL supports SWE->DEN as the strongest northern play and also avoids leaving BAL open uselessly.
experiences.tasks: Gain the most supply centres in Diplomacy through strategic orders and alliances.

experiences.intentions:
TURKEY: Turkey is down to 3 centers and under severe pressure. Italy is the main immediate threat, occupying BUL and CON with fleets in ION/GRE/TYS and dislodging F CON; Russia in SEV/GAL/BUD is also dangerous but less immediate in this retreat. This phase only concerns the dislodged fleet from CON, with legal options SMY or disband. Preserving a unit is critical, and retreating to SMY keeps fleet count higher, preserves defensive flexibility on the southern coast, and avoids an immediate permanent loss.
ENGLAND: We are at 5 centers with only 3 standing units plus 2 dislodged fleets; preserving force count is critical. France is the immediate tactical enemy since both retreats are from western/frontline positions, while Germany is a major strategic threat at 6 centers adjacent to North Sea/Low Countries, so keeping naval presence near home waters matters most. Retreat rather than disband both if possible: F BEL to ENG is strongest for home defense and pressure on France; F MAO to NAO preserves Atlantic leverage and avoids trapping the fleet on the continent.
ITALY: Italy is strong at 9 centers with a broad Balkan/Eastern Med position; this is a retreat phase with only dislodged A BUD to resolve. Russia is the main immediate land threat in the Balkans, while Turkey remains a secondary eastern concern and Austria is nearly eliminated. Since retreat options are limited to SER or disband, preserving the unit is clearly best: Serbia is one of our supply centers and keeps force concentration on the key Balkan front.
experiences.tasks: Gain the most supply centres in Diplomacy through strategic orders and alliances.
